codeReview.summary: Clean, correct implementation of PL031 RTC wall-clock across three files (kernel, init, compositor). The kernel discovers PL031 from DTB using the existing device_tree parser, appends it to the device manifest with a pseudo device_id (200). Init recognizes it, skips driver spawning, saves the PA, and sends it to the compositor via MSG_RTC_CONFIG. Compositor maps the MMIO page and reads the DR register on each timer tick. Fallback to boot-elapsed counter when RTC is unavailable. All reads use volatile pointers (correct for MMIO). The RtcConfig struct is repr(C) and fits within the 60-byte IPC payload. The epoch-to-UTC conversion in format_time_hms is correct. Two non-blocking concerns noted below.

issues.description: Message ordering fragility: The compositor receives optional init messages in a hardcoded sequential order using try_recv + type check. If any intermediate optional message (IMAGE_CONFIG, ICON_CONFIG, IMG_ICON_CONFIG) is not sent by init, the RTC_CONFIG message will be consumed by an earlier try_recv call and silently discarded because its type won't match. Currently works because init always sends all intermediate messages (font/image assets are always loaded). But if a future change makes any intermediate message conditional without sending it, the RTC config will be silently lost. Consider adding a message-dispatch loop or at least a comment documenting the required send order.

Semantic mismatch: PL031 RTC info is stored in VirtioDeviceInfo struct despite not being a virtio device. The struct fields (pa, irq, device_id) happen to work (irq=0, device_id=200), but the naming is misleading. The existing code pattern accommodates it well and a rename would touch many files, so this is acceptable for now.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md documents IPC message type allocation as 'Types 1-7 = config messages' but the actual allocation now includes type 9 (MSG_IMG_ICON_CONFIG) and type 15 (MSG_RTC_CONFIG) as config messages. The convention range should be updated to reflect reality (e.g., 'Types 1-15 = config messages').
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md says 'Types 1-7 = config messages' but system/services/compositor/main.rs lines 38-42 show config message types 3, 6, 7, 9, 15. Type 9 predated this feature (already out of range), type 15 was added by this feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The fragile sequential try_recv pattern for optional init→compositor messages is an implicit protocol constraint not documented anywhere. The send order in init must exactly match the receive order in compositor. A convention note or code comment documenting this invariant would prevent future bugs.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: init/main.rs sends: COMPOSITOR_CONFIG → IMAGE_CONFIG → ICON_CONFIG → IMG_ICON_CONFIG → RTC_CONFIG. compositor/main.rs receives in exactly the same order at lines 919, 1053, 1122, 1225, 1304. Any ordering mismatch silently drops messages.

summary: PASS. The PL031 RTC integration is correctly implemented across the full kernel→init→compositor pipeline. DTB discovery, device manifest forwarding, MMIO mapping, volatile register reads, and epoch-to-UTC conversion are all sound. Visual verification in the handoff confirms the clock matches host UTC within 3 seconds. No new tests were added, which is acceptable — the feature is pure integration with no host-testable algorithmic logic (format_time_hms already existed). Two non-blocking issues: (1) fragile sequential message ordering could silently drop RTC config if intermediate messages aren't sent in the future, (2) VirtioDeviceInfo struct used for a non-virtio device. Two shared-state observations: AGENTS.md IPC type ranges are outdated, and the init→compositor message ordering invariant is undocumented.
